metadata.scenario: A daughter calls extremely upset because the home health aide didn't show up this morning. Her mother missed medication. She demands a supervisor callback within the hour or she's switching agencies. Very angry tone throughout.

transcription.text: Thank you for calling CareFirst Home Health, this is Margaret. How can I assist you today? Margaret, this is Charles Thompson. I'm really upset right now! The aide was supposed to come this morning and didn’t show up! I'm so sorry to hear that, Charles. Let me gather some details. Can you please confirm your mother’s name? It’s Doris Thompson. She missed her medication because of this! I understand how critical that is. Let me look up her care schedule right away. This is absolutely unacceptable! She relies on that support, and now I have to scramble to make sure she gets her meds! I completely understand your frustration, Charles. I’ll do everything I can to resolve this. Just a moment while I pull up her information... Okay, I see that a caregiver was scheduled but it seems they didn’t check in. Didn’t check in? That's ridiculous! I need a supervisor to call me back within the hour or I’m switching agencies! I hear you, and I certainly don’t want to lose your trust. I will connect you with our Client Services Manager, Angela. I’ll also make sure she’s aware of your concerns about the missed medication. You better! This isn’t the first time something like this has happened. I apologize for the repeated issues. I’ll ensure Angela calls you right after this call. I need assurance this won’t happen again. Absolutely. We will review this situation to prevent future occurrences. In the meantime, would you like assistance coordinating any urgent care right now? I don’t know, maybe. Just get me that call back! Of course, I’ll prioritize the callback. Would you like me to send a nurse to check on your mother today? Yes, if you can do that, I would appreciate it. I'll arrange for a nurse to visit. We can offer skilled nursing care, which includes medication management. How much is that? It ranges from $95 to $125 for an hour, depending on the specific needs. Fine. Just make sure someone comes. I’ll handle it personally, Charles. Expect a call from Angela shortly, and I’ll ensure a nurse is dispatched. Alright, thank you. Thank you for your patience, Charles. We value your family and apologize for the inconvenience. I hope this gets sorted out. It will be. Take care, and you’ll hear from us soon. Goodbye. Goodbye.
